context.description: A philosophical discussion of perceived free will in eye movement and pattern recognition. The author reconstructs a timeline of seeing the number 666 on a passing car and argues that awareness and eye motion occur after the pattern has already entered the visual field.
normalization_notes: Classified as body-content theoretical discussion based on reflective prose about perception and free will.
Book title inferred from the source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: Reflection on whether the decision to look was truly voluntary, followed by a timeline of visual perception and eye movement while noticing the number 666 on a passing car.
The analysis estimates short intervals between the car entering the field of view, the pattern becoming visible, the rear window being obscured, and conscious awareness of the number.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2. Page 21 discusses perceived free will, pattern recognition, eye movement, visual perception, and a timing analysis centered on the number 666.
